context.observed_problem: Windows are sometimes opened while mechanical cooling is active.

evidence.text: Simultaneous window opening and mechanical cooling may increase cooling demand and contribute to avoidable energy use.
Timely and understandable feedback can reduce information asymmetry and help occupants make better operational decisions.
Behavioral interventions should remain transparent, non-mandatory, and easy to ignore or opt out of.
Ventilation decisions should consider outdoor temperature and air quality rather than treating every window-opening event as inappropriate.

targets.target_statement: Reduce unnecessary simultaneous window opening and mechanical cooling.

evidence.claim: Simultaneous window opening and mechanical cooling may increase cooling demand and contribute to avoidable energy use.

targets.priority_reason: The issue has a clear operational consequence and can be addressed with a lightweight, non-mandatory intervention.
uncertainties: Outdoor temperature and air-quality conditions are not fully known.

interventions.title: Context-aware window-HVAC reminder
interventions.message: Cooling is active while this window is open. Consider closing the window when outdoor conditions do not require ventilation.
interventions.trigger_condition: A window is open while HVAC cooling is active; outdoor temperature and air-quality conditions must also be considered before deployment.
implementation_steps: Detect simultaneous window opening and mechanical cooling.
Check whether outdoor conditions make window ventilation necessary.
Display a short and transparent reminder.
Allow occupants to ignore or dismiss the reminder.

evidence.claim: Simultaneous window opening and mechanical cooling may increase cooling demand and contribute to avoidable energy use.
Timely and understandable feedback can reduce information asymmetry and help occupants make better operational decisions.
Behavioral interventions should remain transparent, non-mandatory, and easy to ignore or opt out of.
Ventilation decisions should consider outdoor temperature and air quality rather than treating every window-opening event as inappropriate.

risks: The reminder may be inappropriate when outdoor ventilation is needed.
Repeated reminders may cause notification fatigue.
verification_questions: Does the reminder explain why it appears?
Can occupants easily ignore or dismiss it?
Does the trigger account for relevant outdoor conditions?

interventions.title: Energy consequence feedback
interventions.message: An open window during mechanical cooling may increase avoidable cooling demand. Review outdoor conditions before choosing an action.
interventions.trigger_condition: A window and mechanical cooling remain active at the same time for a defined monitoring period.
implementation_steps: Estimate and describe the likely operational consequence without claiming an exact energy saving.
Send the feedback through the dashboard or mobile notification.
Let occupants dismiss the message without penalty.

evidence.claim: Simultaneous window opening and mechanical cooling may increase cooling demand and contribute to avoidable energy use.
Timely and understandable feedback can reduce information asymmetry and help occupants make better operational decisions.
Behavioral interventions should remain transparent, non-mandatory, and easy to ignore or opt out of.
Ventilation decisions should consider outdoor temperature and air quality rather than treating every window-opening event as inappropriate.

risks: A general consequence message may overstate impact in some conditions.
Notifications may be ignored if they are too frequent.
verification_questions: Is the consequence stated without unsupported numerical claims?
Is the message understandable and non-mandatory?
Can the user opt out of future notifications?

feedback.requested_change: Explicitly include outdoor temperature and air-quality conditions.
